title: Procedural Validation
text: Procedural Validation In procedural validation, the data is checked to ensure it conforms to constraints that are defined in the validation triggers. For example, a procedural constraint can parse a citizen's identity number to obtain a date of birth. This date of birth can then be used to allow or disallow the data. Uniface handles procedural validation by executing the ProcScript in the validate triggers of fields and entities, and the validateKey trigger of entities. These triggers are intended for implementing field-level and entity-level business rules. (It is recommended to define these triggers in the modeled entities.) They are not in available in Report components because Uniface reports are read-only, so no data is modified. Related Links trigger validateKey System trigger that defines the procedural checks to validate a modified primary or candidate key. trigger validate (Entity) System trigger for validating entity occurrences. trigger validate (Field) System trigger that defines the procedural checks to validate a modified field.
